출처: TABLE 10.7 Quality Measures for Thermal Oxide Layer
표 유형: quality

[품질항목]
1. Oxide thickness.

[결함유형]
A. Thickness of gate oxide outside of specification (representative gate oxide specification is 20 ± 1.5 Å).

[비고]
Possible reasons for this problem are:
- Incorrect process gas flow (e.g., MFC calibrated improperly). Since HCl enhances oxidation rate, verify HCl:O₂ ratio is correct.
- Verify O₂ leak integrity of the furnace with a bare silicon test wafer.
- Check the metrology equipment by measuring the oxide thickness against a standard thickness wafer.
- Excess native oxide growth due to overexposure of wafer to air either before or after normal furnace oxidation.

[품질항목]
2. Gate oxide integrity (GOI).

[결함유형]
A. Gate oxide breakdown.
B. Mobile ionic contamination (MIC) in film.

[비고]
Gate oxide defects are often related to processing conditions:
- Perform a C-V test to demonstrate gate oxide integrity using an unpatterned test wafer.
- Perform an oxide-charge analysis on a test wafer with a surface-charge analyzer.
- Review the preoxidation clean steps to assess sources of contamination (e.g., particles or MICs).
- Verify no contamination from an incoming gas line or defective filter.

[결함유형]
A. Contaminated quartzware.
B. Wafer broken inside furnace.
C. Contaminated carrier.
D. Contaminated gas filter or line.

[비고]
Actions to correct these sources of particles added during the oxidation process are:
- Check cleanliness of quartzware and carrier.
- Verify the alignment of the robotic handling systems.
- Check incoming gas filters.

출처: TABLE 10.8 Common Oxidation Troubleshooting Problems
표 유형: troubleshooting

[고장모드]
1. Incorrect gas flow into furnace tube.

[원인]
A. Incorrect process recipe.
B. Malfunctioning MFC.
C. Incorrect H₂:O₂ ratio for steam process (O₂ starved).

[조치]
Possible corrective actions for this problem are:
- Verify that correct process recipe is downloaded into furnace software for the wafer being processed.
- Check process gas MFCs (O₂, N₂, H₂, Cl) to verify calibration was performed and operating properly.
- Verify gas valves are functioning properly (e.g., no leaks, and so on).
- Check that no room air is leaking into the furnace tube from outside the quartz or furnace door seal.

[고장모드]
2. Incorrect temperature uniformity in vertical furnace chamber.

[원인]
A. Wrong process recipe for product.
B. Incorrect operation of thermocouples.

[조치]
Possible corrective actions are:
- Verify that the correct process recipe is loaded for the wafer being processed.
- Assess whether the temperature nonuniformity is during the ramp up, flat zone, and ramp down for corrective action.
- Check all thermocouples for proper operation. Verify no degradation due to excessive heat or corrosion. Replace defective thermocouples. Verify no drift in thermocouple reference temperature.

3. Inadequate temperature uniformity in rapid thermal processor (RTP).

[원인]
A. Malfunctioning heating system (e.g., tungsten halogen lamps).
B. Verify correct operation of temperature measurement sensor.

[조치]
Possible corrective actions are:
- Verify heating lamps are operating and have the correct lamp intensity.
- Verify correct calibration and temperature measurement of the optical pyrometer temperature sensor. Check there is no variation in wafer emissivity by performing reflectivity measurement of the wafer surface.

출처: TABLE 11.7. Key Quality Measures for CVD
표 유형: quality

[품질항목]
1. Voids during deposition of high-aspect ratio gap (>3:1) with PECVD SiO₂

[결함유형]
A. Keyhole voids are formed during deposition of high aspect ratio gaps. After chemical mechanical planarization (CMP) and removal of top surface, some voids become trenches (see Figure 11.31 on page 294). This trench can lead to an open circuit after metal deposition.

[비고]
- Gap fill is critical as CDs are reduced and gaps have a high aspect ratio.
- Voids are highly stressed regions and trap moisture or solvents that cause corrosion or outgas at high vacuum.
- The root cause of this problem is trying to fill a high aspect ratio gap with a PECVD that is limited to deposition system. This application requires HDPCVD.

metadata.title: # 반도체 전공정 5단계 트러블슈팅표
metadata.table: TABLE 11.7. Key Quality Measures for CVD

page_content:
공정: DEPO
출처: TABLE 11.7. Key Quality Measures for CVD
표 유형: quality

[품질항목]
2. Film stress

[결함유형]
A. High film stress leads to cracking and delamination.
B. Film stress can propagate silicon defects in the substrate.
C. Stress may cause current leakage.

[비고]
The presence of dopant in a glass film can reduce stress. Deposition parameters that affect film stress are:
- RF power: adjust power to improve stress (e.g., reduce power by 10 watts to lower stress).
- Pressure: adjust pressure to affect stress (e.g. increased pressure leads to higher film stress).

metadata.title: # 반도체 전공정 5단계 트러블슈팅표
metadata.table: TABLE 11.7. Key Quality Measures for CVD

page_content:
공정: DEPO
출처: TABLE 11.7. Key Quality Measures for CVD
표 유형: quality

[품질항목]
3. Film thickness

[결함유형]
A. Thickness of film exceeds requirements.

[비고]
Deposition parameters that affect thickness are:
- Time: reduce deposition time to reduce thickness (e.g., 1 sec reduction for each 100 Å of thickness).
- Gas-flow rate: reduce gas flow to reduce thickness (e.g., reduce SiH₄ by 2 sccm for >5% thickness over nominal).

metadata.title: # 반도체 전공정 5단계 트러블슈팅표
metadata.table: TABLE 11.7. Key Quality Measures for CVD

page_content:
공정: DEPO
출처: TABLE 11.7. Key Quality Measures for CVD
표 유형: quality

[품질항목]
4. Refractive index (R/I)

[결함유형]
A. R/I is a good monitor to assess quality of the film.
B. R/I is highly dependent on the composition of the deposited film (stoichiometry).

[비고]
R/I is an optical property of a material. Compared to thermal oxides, CVD oxides are inferior in quality and integrity. CVD oxides exhibit more particles and pinholes. The R/I of CVD SiO₂ can be compared to the R/I of SiO₂ (1.46) to assess relative quality:
- High R/I means excessive Si in film.
- Low R/I indicates a porous film, which tends to absorb moisture.

page_content:
공정: DEPO
출처: TABLE 11.8. Common CVD Troubleshooting Problems
표 유형: troubleshooting

[고장모드]
1. Particle contamination associated with film.

[원인]
Source of particles is isolated by determining whether particles are found on top of film, in film, or below film:
A. On film: particles formed after deposition. Check for particles on sidewalls of hot-wall reactors and belt-driven reactors.
B. In film: Gas-phase nucleation particles from gas too rich in silane or silicon. Gas supply contamination causes particles.
C. Under film: Particles from silicon carbide, quartz, or reactor walls fall on wafer before deposition.

[조치]
- Particles on film: Particles on sidewalls indicate need for more frequent wet cleaning of quartzware and chamber surfaces for preventive maintenance. Inspect in situ dry cleaning process. Also verify the correct procedures for cleaning (manually or in situ).
- Particles in film: Improper gas flows from MFC calibration or problem in process recipe of software program. Check for leaks in gas supply system or O-rings. Verify point-of-use filters are acceptable.
- Particles under film: Check wafer cleaning process before deposition.

metadata.title: # 반도체 전공정 5단계 트러블슈팅표
metadata.table: TABLE 11.8. Common CVD Troubleshooting Problems

page_content:
공정: DEPO
출처: TABLE 11.8. Common CVD Troubleshooting Problems
표 유형: troubleshooting

[고장모드]
2. Film thickness.

[원인]
Thickness is related to both equipment and process problems. Variables affecting thickness are:
A. Incorrect temperature control.
B. System pressure is too high or low.
C. System power needs adjustment.
D. Improper gas flow.

[조치]
- Temperature controller may require calibration. A common problem is a defective thermocouple.
- System pressure is controlled by process recipe. Check vacuum system for leaks.
- Adjust RF power to optimize film thickness.
- Check calibration of MFC to ensure proper gas flow.
- If checking thickness with test wafer, verify the test wafer is clean and does not have a thickness variation before the test.

metadata.title: # 반도체 전공정 5단계 트러블슈팅표
metadata.table: TABLE 11.8. Common CVD Troubleshooting Problems

page_content:
공정: DEPO
출처: TABLE 11.8. Common CVD Troubleshooting Problems
표 유형: troubleshooting

[고장모드]
3. Cracking of dielectric material on top of electrostatic chuck (ESC).

[원인]
High thermal load at the ESC causes the dielectric material to erode or crack. This condition leads to plasma arcing or wafer chucking/dechucking problems.

[조치]
- Investigate the ESC backside cooling system to ensure it is functioning properly.
- Inspect ESC materials to verify there is no breakdown due to high power exposure, temperature, or plasma clean conditions.

표 유형: quality

[품질항목]
1. Sputtered metal adhesion.

[결함유형]
A. Metal layer does not adhere to substrate.

[비고]
Parameters affecting film adhesion are:
- Wafer contamination
- Stress
- Types of materials
- Temperature of substrate
- Argon pressure

metadata.title: # 반도체 전공정 5단계 트러블슈팅표
metadata.table: TABLE 12.6 Quality Measures for Metallization

page_content:
공정: DEPO
출처: TABLE 12.6 Quality Measures for Metallization
표 유형: quality

[품질항목]
2. Sputtered film stress.

[결함유형]
A. Excessive stress leads to:
- Cracks in the film surface.
- Loss of film adhesion.
- Increased resistivity in some materials.

[비고]
Film stress can be caused by excessive temperature of the wafer substrate. Ways to reduce wafer temperature are:
- Lower deposition rate
- Increased backside cooling
Cracks can cause:
- Peeling of the film layer
- Contaminant migration
- Electrical opens or short.
Measure film stress (bow of wafer surface) before and after deposition. Units for stress are dynes.

metadata.title: # 반도체 전공정 5단계 트러블슈팅표
metadata.table: TABLE 12.6 Quality Measures for Metallization

page_content:
공정: DEPO
출처: TABLE 12.6 Quality Measures for Metallization
표 유형: quality

[품질항목]
3. Sputtered film thickness.

[결함유형]
A. Metal layer does not meet film thickness specifications (e.g., sheet resistance out of specification).

[비고]
Parameters that affect film thickness are:
- Incorrect recipe
- Improper flow rates
- Improper substrate temperature
- Improper chamber pressure
- Improper power supply energy
- Wrong time setting

metadata.title: # 반도체 전공정 5단계 트러블슈팅표
metadata.table: TABLE 12.6 Quality Measures for Metallization

page_content:
공정: DEPO
출처: TABLE 12.6 Quality Measures for Metallization
표 유형: quality

[품질항목]
4. Uniformity of electroplated (electrochemical deposition, or ECD) metal film.

[결함유형]
A. Nonuniform film thickness indicated by:
- Inadequate gap fill and step coverage for high aspect ratio openings (nonuniform metal thickness at top and bottom of high aspect ratio holes).
- Nonuniform deposition thickness across the wafer and from wafer to wafer.
- Voids in film.

[비고]
Critical parameters for electroplating film uniformity are:
- Depositing a uniform CVD seed layer without voids.
- Maintaining proper electroplating bath chemistry (composition and concentration) for organic additives, primarily brighteners and suppressors, to attain void-free deposition that fills bottom and sidewalls of gaps.

1. Degradation in step coverage of metal film.

[원인]
A. Decrease in substrate temperature. For Al alloy sputter deposition, step coverage is dependent on the wafer temperature during deposition.

[조치]
Heating the substrate to improve step coverage because surface mobility of deposited metal atoms improves.

[원인]
B. Increase in deposition rate.

[조치]

[조치]

- Check for O₂ or N₂ in metal films that can change resistivity and film stress.

[원인]
B. Outgassing or system leaks.

[조치]
- Use helium leak detection to inspect for system leaks.
- Assess the chamber conditions before deposition with residual gas analyzer.

[고장모드]
3. Contamination of metal film.

[원인]
A. Particles on surface of film.

[조치]
Check the following common particle sources:
- Dirty input wafers.
- Problems in hand-off between robot and chamber mechanism.
- Incomplete chamber cleaning between runs.
- Dirty cassettes.
- Contaminated nitrogen backfill.

metadata.title: # 반도체 전공정 5단계 트러블슈팅표

page_content:
공정: DEPO
출처: TABLE 12.7 Common Metallization Troubleshooting Problems
표 유형: troubleshooting

[고장모드]
4. Voids in copper trench fill after dual-damascene electroplating.

[원인]
A. Excessive seed layer thickness on the wafer surface (field) that pinches off the via or trench, creating a center void in the film.*

[조치]
Optimize the CVD Cu seed layer deposition by evaluating the electroplating current from the field to the bottom of vias and trenches. The goal is to deposit adequate seed layer at the bottom of a high-aspect ratio feature without increasing field thickness.

metadata.title: # 반도체 전공정 5단계 트러블슈팅표
metadata.table: TABLE 12.7 Common Metallization Troubleshooting Problems

page_content:
공정: DEPO
출처: TABLE 12.7 Common Metallization Troubleshooting Problems
표 유형: troubleshooting

[고장모드]
5. Excessive copper dishing after dual-damascene CMP.

[원인]
A. Cu dishing for Cu CMP is often caused by tantalum diffusion barrier, which must be polished back in the presence of Cu.

[조치]
After Cu is polished, the Ta layer must be removed from the level. The Cu pad/slurry field does not effectively remove Ta.
Options are:
- Optimize the pad/slurry for Ta.
- Minimize the Ta field level thickness.

출처: TABLE 13.6 Key Quality Measures for Photoresist
표 유형: quality

[품질항목]
1. Resist adhesion.

[결함유형]
A. Resist dewetting, which is also called lift-off. The resist does not adhere to the substrate, causing problems during subsequent etch or ion implantation processing.

[비고]
Possible causes of resist lift-off are:
- Contamination on wafer surface.
- Inadequate HMDS priming or dehydration bake resulting in moisture on wafer surface (measured by contact angle meter as described in Chapter 7).
- Excessive HMDS priming can cause resist "popping," which is failure of poorly adhered resist.
- SiO₂ is a difficult surface to achieve good adhesion because it is a hydrophilic surface (water attracting).*

page_content:
공정: LITHO
출처: TABLE 13.6 Key Quality Measures for Photoresist

[품질항목]

[품질항목]
3. Thickness of resist coating.

[결함유형]
A. Coated resist thickness is out of control. Resist thickness must be uniform: wafer-to-wafer mean thickness requirement is often <30 Å total indicated runout (TIR, which is maximum minus minimum thickness).

[비고]
Parameters that affect thickness of resist are:
- Check spin acceleration and speed. Higher spin speeds produce thinner resist (recall that thickness is inversely proportional to the square root of spin speed). Verify spin speed versus thickness characteristic curve of resist manufacturer. At low spin speeds, irregular solvent loss leads to thickness nonuniformity.
- Verify correct spin time. Resist reaches stable thickness in several seconds and requires additional time for thickness uniformity.
- Check for correct resist type and viscosity.
- Verify no mechanical vibration or air turbulence during high speed spin dry.

[고장모드]
1. Excessive resist usage during spin coating (DUV resist costs $2,000 to $5,000 per gallon).

[원인]
A. Sub-optimum setting of process variables during spin coating process.

[조치]
Optimize process variables for spin coating:
- First, determine minimum volume of resist needed to coat the wafer (referred to as cut-off volume).
- Second, evaluate the following critical parameters using design of experiments: dispense spin speed, exhaust flow rate during dispense and drying steps, dispense rate, resist temperature, cool plate temperature, and ambient air temperature.

2. Wafers damaged (broken or chipped) during normal processing in the wafer track.

[원인]
A. Improper setup of different tools used in track for processing wafers.

[조치]
Check the following equipment for proper setup:
- Improper calibration of robot arms during wafer handoff from one track tool to another.
- Loss of chuck vacuum during spin.
- Spin coat machine is not leveled properly and has mechanical vibration.
- Excessive spin speed.
- Verify calibration of cassette indexer to index wafers in cassette for robot pickup.

[고장모드]
3. DNQ-novolak resist becomes unstable and contaminated with articles.

[원인]
A. DNQ-novolak resist has aged due to shelf life.

[조치]
Excessive shelf life of DNQ-novolak resist (> several months, with maximum of six months to one year) can lead to the following:\*\*
- Increase in absorption at longer wavelengths.
- Susceptible to thermal degradation of the DNQ, leading to crosslinking and increase of high molecular-weight resist components.
- Formation of undesirable acids in the resist.
- Precipitation (falling out of solution) of sensitizer to form crystallized particles that contaminate resists. This especially occurs at high-temperature storage.
- Point-of-use filtration is common practice for production applications to control resist consistency. Typical filter size is 0.05 μm for 0.25 μm feature size.

[결함유형]
A. Incorrect focus-exposure for system. Conduct focus-exposure optimization test while measuring CD linewidth.

[비고]
- Verify uniform and optimum exposure from illumination source.
- Make CD measurements of line versus a series of exposure dose values for a nominal focal position (e.g., 30% from top surface).
- With nominal focal position, find optimum dose for producing CD.
- Modify focal position and conduct CD measurements. Wide range of acceptable dose exposure at optimum focal position.
- Verify the bulk resist meets all quality parameters.

[결함유형]
A. Poor resolution for CDs on wafer: linewidths and holes do not meet specification.

[비고]
Resolution is often a focus problem:
- Perform focus-exposure test.
- Check environment (temperature, pressure).
- Wafer is not flat on chuck, possibly due to backside contamination or chuck problem.
- Look for possible incoming process-related problems or improper process parameters or reticle.
- Look for optics problems (e.g., lens aberrations).

표 유형: quality

[품질항목]
5. Reticle quality.

[결함유형]
The following are reticle defects:
A. Dirt or scratches on reticle.
B. Pattern defects on reticles:
  - Break in line.
  - Bridge between features.
  - Missing geometry.
  - Opaque spot from isolated chrome.
  - Pinhole in chrome line.
C. Glass fracture.
D. Lifted chrome (poor adhesion).
E. Reticle plate flatness.

[비고]
- Scratches can remove chrome and cause defect in resist.
- A break in line extends completely across the chrome feature.
- Bridge will join two chrome features across a clear space on the reticle.
- Missing geometry is a pattern not on reticle, such as a missing contact.
- Opaque spot is an area of chrome that should not be on reticle.
- Pinhole is a hole in a chrome pattern.
- Reticles should not have fractures and be controlled for flatness and warpage.

- Wavelength stability.

[품질항목]
1. Critical dimension bias.

[결함유형]
A. Linewidth change: excessive difference between photoresist linewidth and final feature linewidth after etch.

[비고]
- CD bias for linewidth change is measured by comparing pre-etch linewidth in photoresist with post-etch linewidth of the same feature on the wafer. Measurements can be done with an SEM.
- Excessive CD bias requires optimization of the etch process for undercutting or slope.
- The photoresist profile has an effect on CD bias. Vertical resist profile produces best CD bias.

2. Metal corrosion.

[결함유형]
A. Corrosion or attack of metal film after etch.

[비고]
- Corrosion often results from residual HCl on the wafer that is exposed to water vapor in the air.
- Corrosion may be visible as small bubbles along the side of metal lines. This defect is looked for by optical microscope or SEM.

[결함유형]

표 유형: quality

[품질항목]
6. Excessive post-etch residue.

[결함유형]
A. The following types of residue may exist after etch:
- Stringers (small strings of residue)
- Veils (thin residue overhang)
- Crowns
- Rails
- Corrosion after metal etch

[비고]
The causes of residue after etch are:
- Nonuniform etch process.
- Films deposited over topography.
- Nonuniform dopant distribution in film.
- Contaminants in the film (in addition to intentional alloys such as Cu in Si).
- Contaminants in gas or chamber. Check supply filters or clean system.
- Incorrect process parameter, such as high etch rate.

[품질항목]
1. 웨이퍼 표면의 긁힘과 둥근 홀들

[결함유형]
A. 내부 금속 단락(금속층과 금속층)

[비고]
미세흠집들은 정밀조사에서 찾아내기가 극도로 힘들다.
가능한 원인:
- 슬러리 안으로 확산되는 입자 크기의 빈약한 제어
- 오랜 축적과 부적절한 사용은 슬러리 입자의 건조를 야기하고, 더 큰 입자 만들게 한다.
- 슬러리에서의 입자오염
- 미세흠집을 줄이기 위한 이차 완충 작용의 결핍 혹은 최적화되지 않았음

metadata.title: # 반도체 전공정 5단계 트러블슈팅표

page_content:

출처: 표 18-6 CMP의 품질 측정
표 유형: quality

[품질항목]
2. Dishing
(사발 모양으로 움푹 들어가는 것)

[결함유형]
A. 높은 연마 비율 물질에서의 침하(지반의 함몰)

[비고]
- dishing은 전형적으로 낮은 연마 비율의 환경(예, 연마 정지층으로 사용되는 질화물) 안에 있는 높은 연마 비율 물질들(예, 산화 STI)에서 발생한다.
- 높은 연마 비율 물질이 보다 큰 표면적을 가졌을 때 더 많은 dishing이 발생한다.
- PAD의 단단함의 증가는 dishing을 줄일 것이다(PAD는 덜 깊숙한 곳에서 구부러진다).
- 임시방편으로서 얼마간의 디자이너들은 큰 것 안에서 dishing을 막기 위해 버팀 PAD로써 견본을 사용한다(예, bonding pads). 이것은 표면이 이용 가능할 때만 이용된다.
- 구리(Cu)에서, hard pad와 높은 화학적 제거도를 갖는 슬러리와의 결합(오로지 높은 구역에서만)은 dishing을 최소화할 것이다.

3. 부식(침식)

[결함유형]
A. 낮은 연마 비율 물질에서 침하 또는 과도한 제거

[비고]
- 낮은 연마 비율 물질(예, 질화물)의 부식
- 그것은 금속 패턴 밀도가 질화물 차폐와 텅스텐을 상호 연결할 때와 같이 증가할 때 두드러진다.
- 비아 식각이 하부 금속층과 연결되지 않을 때 불완전한 비아 시각을 야기시킬 수 있다.
- 일반 압축성과 최적 조건의 슬러리 이동을 위해 조정할 수 있는 연마 패드들을 연구하라.

[품질항목]
4. 잔류물(찌꺼기)

[결함유형]
A. 모서리를 따라서 스트링거의 구성에서의 잔류물

[비고]
CMP는 상대적인 세정 공정이다(RIE etch back 공정과 비교될 때). 적절히 수행된 CMP는 특정한 모서리를 따라서 스트링거들을 줄일 것이다. 스트링거들은 단락을 야기시키거나 칩의 신뢰성을 감하시킨다. 스트링거들의 가능한 요인과 작용들:
- 스트링거들은 텅스텐과 폴리실리콘 stringer와 같은 다른 물질들에서 발생한다.
- 연마 패드와 슬러리 화학물질 등을 최대한 활용해서 다른 물질들 사이에서 더 많은 단일화된 연마를 이루어서 스트링거들을 최소화하라.

공정: CMP
출처: 표 18-7 CMP 고장진단 문제
표 유형: troubleshooting

[고장모드]
1. 슬러리 안의 과도하게 큰 입자들

[원인]
A. 슬러리가 디스펜서 측벽의 내부에서 건조되고, 슬러리 혼합물 안으로 떨어진다.
B. 입력 슬러리에서의 마찰 입자의 럼핑
C. 공급업자에 의해서 제공되는 불량 슬러리

[조치]
- POU 필터 여과법을 슬러리에 사용해라.
- 슬러리의 제조와 납품을 최적화하라.
- 계면활성제와 안정제로 슬러리의 안정성을 향상시켜라.

metadata.title: # 반도체 전공정 5단계 트러블슈팅표
metadata.table: 표 18-7 CMP 고장진단 문제

page_content:
공정: CMP
출처: 표 18-7 CMP 고장진단 문제
표 유형: troubleshooting

[고장모드]
2. 웨이퍼 표면의 불균일한 연마

[원인]
A. 중심을 빠르게(중심을 웨이퍼의 다른 지역보다 빠르게 연마하라).
B. 중심을 느리게(중심을 웨이퍼의 다른 지역보다 느리게 연마하라).

[조치]
- 플래튼 위의 부적절한 패드 외형은 중심을 빠르게 또는 느리게 하거나, 가장자리 문제들을 야기할 수 있다. 과도하게 패드가 닳았는지 검사하라.
- 웨이퍼 캐리어(wafer carrier) 위의 압력(아래 방향 압력)이 잘못 설정되어 있다.
- 패드 콘디션 암(arm)이 적절하게 적용되었는지 또는 닳았는지(glazing 안 된 상태) 검사하라.
- 부족한 슬러리 흐름률 또는 잘못된 점성률(흐르는 정도).
- 웨이퍼 캐리어에서 후면막의 악화는 웨이퍼가 납작하게 펼쳐지지 않게 야기한다.
- 테이블의 회전 속도가 잘못 설정되어 있다.
- 웨이퍼의 뒤쪽 압력이 잘못 설정되어 있다.

metadata.title: # 반도체 전공정 5단계 트러블슈팅표
metadata.table: 표 18-7 CMP 고장진단 문제

page_content:
공정: CMP
출처: 표 18-7 CMP 고장진단 문제
표 유형: troubleshooting

[고장모드]
3. 구리 CMP 후에 깨끗한 웨이퍼의 입자들

[원인]
A. 웨이퍼에서 과도한 입자들의 발견(0.18 μm CD에서 요구되는 것은 0.08 μm 크기에서 웨이퍼당 결함들이 20 미만이다).
B. 광 직렬 자국들과 bond 패드 지역들과 같이 깊은 특성들 안에서 잔여의 슬러리의 생성.
C. 유전체 또는 패턴화된 웨이퍼들의 line들 사이로부터 잔여 구리의 제거

[조치]
CMP의 주된 관심은 세정후의 표면에서의 입자들의 레벨이다. 가능한 해결 방법들:
- 잔여 구리의 세정이 패턴화된 웨이퍼에서 끝났는지 아닌지를 조사하라.
- 양쪽 브러시 솔(수세미)에서 브러시가 적재되고 있는지 검사하라(오염).
- 특히 구리 잔여물에 최적의 세정 화학제가 사용되었는지 확인하라.
- 웨이퍼 표면에 다른 물질을 노출시키는 것부터 브러시와 세정 화학물질까지 화학적으로 역 오염이 없다는 것을 확신하라.

metadata.title: # 반도체 전공정 5단계 트러블슈팅표

page_content:
공정: CMP
출처: 표 18-7 CMP 고장진단 문제
표 유형: troubleshooting

[고장모드]
4. 연마 패드의 윤내기

[원인]
A. 부적절하게 적용된 검사용 암
B. 과도하게 닳은 패드

[조치]
패드 시운전과 검사는 일관된 연마 수행을 위해 필요하다.
윤내기 문제 해결들:
- 적절한 패드 시운전이 수행되었는지 확인하라(예, 생산 웨이퍼를 가동하기에 앞서 견본 웨이퍼를 가동하라).
- 하향압력 조사를 적용하거나 검사 표면을 바꿔라.
- 연마패드를 교체하라.

page_content:
## Center pattern
This defect manifests as a concentrated cluster of defective dies located near the wafer's geometric center. Based on the manufacturing process data, Center defects are typically linked to irregularities during thin-film deposition and chemical mechanical polishing (CMP) stages. These process variations can cause uneven film thickness or surface defects specifically in the central wafer region, leading to yield loss in this critical area.

page_content:
## Center pattern
Center defects often result from process non-uniformities in the central zone, especially during thin film deposition or planarization steps such as chemical mechanical polishing. These processes may induce thickness gradients or particle accumulations near the chuck center, leading to concentrated die failures in the core region.

## Center pattern — Thin-Film Deposition
Thin-Film Deposition: Variations or non-uniformities in thin-film deposition can cause material thickness inconsistencies, particularly in the central region of the wafer. This leads to localized defects as observed in the Center pattern.

## Center pattern — Chemical Mechanical Polishing
Chemical Mechanical Polishing (CMP): Inadequate or uneven CMP can result in surface topography variations and defects concentrated at the wafer's center. CMP process control is critical for maintaining uniform surface planarity, especially in the central area.

## Center pattern — Photolithography Alignment
Photolithography Alignment: Misalignment during photolithography, especially near the wafer center, can cause pattern distortions or overlay errors, contributing to defect formation in this region.

page_content:
## Edge-Ring pattern
The Edge-Ring defect appears as a ring-shaped pattern of defective dies near the wafer's outer edge. It is commonly associated with issues in etching and edge-bead removal processes. Deficiencies or inconsistencies in these process steps often result in contamination or damage along the wafer perimeter, producing the characteristic peripheral defect distribution. This type of defect can significantly reduce the functional yield of edge dies.

page_content:
## Edge-Ring pattern
Edge-Ring defects typically arise from etching non-uniformities, edge bead removal failures, or chemical residue accumulation at the wafer periphery. This ring-shaped failure pattern at the outer edge reflects common issues in coating, cleaning, or plasma exposure steps, where equipment tolerances often vary near the boundary.

page_content:
## Scratch pattern — Chemical Mechanical Polishing
Chemical Mechanical Polishing: Scratches frequently originate during the chemical mechanical polishing step when the pad condition degrades or abrasive particles accumulate in the slurry. Localized over-pressure or particle agglomeration can cause the polishing pad to introduce directional scratches.

page_content:
## Scratch pattern — Cleaning
Contamination during cleaning operations: Residual particles or insufficient rinsing in wet or dry cleaning modules may drag across the wafer surface during spin or drying processes, leaving scratch-like traces. Particle shedding from aging components in the cleaning system is also a known contributor.
